steps.title: Identify the key contrast word
steps.content: The word diverged in “Johnson's self-effacement diverged from the editorial stances of W.E.B. Du Bois and Alain Locke” signals a fundamental opposition. Their editorial approaches were the opposite of his self-effacing strategy.

steps.title: Select the word for the opposite editorial approach
steps.content: Dogmatic — stubbornly asserting one's own principles — directly describes the opposite of self-effacement in editorial practice. The others don't work: proficient addresses skill, ambiguous suggests confusion, and unpretentious matches Johnson's modesty. That matches choice B — B is correct.
